items.title: BREAKING: The 3x leveraged long semiconductor ETF, $SOXL, attracted a record +$1.03 billion in inflows on Tuesday. At the same time, the 3x leveraged ...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: The 3x leveraged long semiconductor ETF, $SOXL, attracted a record +$1.03 billion in inflows on Tuesday.

At the same time, the 3x leveraged short semiconductor ETF, $SOXS, posted -$230 million in outflows, the largest daily outflow since late March.

Furthermore, the 3x leveraged long Nasdaq 100 ETF, $TQQQ, saw +$161 million in inflows, the highest daily intake since March 31.

In other words, $SOXL inflows were more than 6 times larger than $TQQQ inflows, as investors concentrated their bullish bets in semiconductors.

$SOXL is now up +354% since the March 30th bottom, the largest 31-day increase since the fund launched in 2010.

This comes as the semiconductor sector, $SOX, is up +68% over the same period, the 3rd-best 31-day performance on record.

The run in semiconductor stocks is truly historic.

items.description_html:
BREAKING: The average price of ground beef in the US is now up to a record $6.90 per pound.

On a non-seasonally adjusted basis, ground beef prices have exceeded $7.00 per pound for the first time in history.

Prices have surged +77% since January 2020, when they stood at $3.89 per pound.

Furthermore, the average price of uncooked beef steaks is up to a record $13.02 per pound, surging +70% since January 2020.

Ground beef prices have now DOUBLED since 2013.

Food inflation is running hotter than ever.

items.title: Silver’s outperformance of gold is gaining momentum: The gold-to-silver ratio is down to 54, the 2nd-lowest since February 2023. This ratio has falle...
items.description_html:
Silver’s outperformance of gold is gaining momentum:

The gold-to-silver ratio is down to 54, the 2nd-lowest since February 2023.

This ratio has fallen -9 points over the last 6 trading days, with silver outperforming gold for 6 consecutive sessions, the longest streak since December 2025.

Over this period, silver prices have surged +20%, to $87 per ounce, the highest since March 11th, while gold prices have increased just +3%, to $4,690 per ounce.

The ratio is now down -51 points, or -49%, since the April 2025 peak of 105 points.

By comparison, the ratio briefly touched 43 points, the lowest level in 15 years, in January 2026, after silver prices surpassed $120 per ounce for the first time in history.

Keep watching precious metals.

items.title: India is apparently considering removing tax on fiis investing in Indian bonds. This is a good thing. Why? Foreign investors pay tax on all capital ga...
items.description_html:
India is apparently considering removing tax on fiis investing in Indian bonds. This is a good thing. Why?

Foreign investors pay tax on all capital gains made in India. India is different from all major economies who follow residence based taxation meaning capital gains are taxed in the country of your residence not the country inwhich you invest. If you buy a US listed stocks and sell it at a profit , from India., the US does not tax you (india does)

For bonds it is a pain because you get taxed for a capital gain at 20% in India. The fpi may be an index fund buying a bond index in which Indian securities are, and they will underperform that index because the tax is deducted in India at source. Bonds incur capital gains when interest rates fall, and if they are sold the fpi pays. Note that an Indian institution like a mutual fund , insurance co or a pension fund pays no tax.

Also the interest is complex. When you buy a bond in the secondary market you pay the price plus the accrued interest from the bond after its last interest payment date. Apparently fpis get hit because even getting paid that accrued interest when they sell a bond has that tax, and ifntheir jurisdictions don't tax mutual funds or pension funds there is no corresponding tax in their home countries to offset what's paid in India. Sadly even if there's only a 5% tax on interest i think the tds is 20%, which sucks double big time cos you have to wait about a year to get a refund.

India should get moving with this and make capital gains and interest on listed bonds paid to fpis totally tax free on cap gains and only 5% tds on interest (or none at all)

We should also eliminate cap gains taxes on stocks for them also just like we don't tax indian institutions on such gains. This is how it is internationally.

It won't reverse fpi flows really but in the long term it will attract foreign capital and allow the rupee to internationalise.

items.description_html:
Apollo Tyres delivered strong Q4 with 14.2% revenue growth and 153 bps margin expansion, driven by favorable raw material dynamics and operating leverage. Significant deleveraging (debt down 22%, D/E at 0.16x) and strong cash generation (FCF ₹2,264 Cr) provide balance sheet strength.

Europe restructuring is on track for June 2026 closure with benefits expected H2 FY27. Key concerns include Americas segment loss and exceptional charges, but normalized PAT of ₹10,650 Cr shows solid operational performance.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 14.2% YoY to ₹7,336 Cr in Q4 FY26

EBITDA margin expanded 153 bps YoY to 14.57%

Debt reduced 22% from ₹3,377 Cr to ₹2,632 Cr, D/E improved to 0.16x from 0.23x

Operating cash flow doubled to ₹36,674 Cr, CFO/PAT at 2.67x

Free cash flow strong at ₹2,264 Cr

APMEA EBIT surged 81.5% YoY with margin at 11.4%

Finance costs declined 17.9% YoY due to deleveraging

Interest coverage improved to 11.87x from 7.37x

items.title: DBL reported mixed Q4 FY26 results with order inflow exceeding guidance but revenue missing targets. The company faces margin pressure in EPC segment ...
items.description_html:
DBL reported mixed Q4 FY26 results with order inflow exceeding guidance but revenue missing targets. The company faces margin pressure in EPC segment (-256 bps YoY) and working capital stress (negative CFO of ₹256.5 Cr standalone).

The DBL 2.0 strategy is showing results with Annuity Projects growing 50% YoY, providing recurring revenue visibility. However, debt reduction targets were missed and operational profitability declined 6.22% YoY on normalized basis.

Positive Factors

Order inflow of ₹17,500+ Cr exceeded guidance of ₹12,000-15,000 Cr by 16-46%

Annuity Projects revenue grew 50.38% YoY to ₹85,554 Lakh, validating DBL 2.0 strategy

Sequential revenue recovery at 7.58% QoQ suggests normalization in execution pace

Consolidated debt/equity improved to 1.16x from 1.81x YoY due to InvIT transfers

Asset divestments to InvIT generated ₹88,046 Lakh exceptional gains in FY26

Risk Factors

Standalone revenue of ₹7,005 Cr missed original guidance of ₹8,000-8,500 Cr

Standalone CFO negative at ₹256.5 Cr; trade receivables up ₹48,967 Lakh

EPC segment margin contracted 256 bps YoY to 15.54% due to input cost pressure

Normalized PAT declined 6.22% YoY (vs reported +66.49% due to exceptional items)

Debt reduction target of ₹500 Cr missed; standalone debt increased 16% YoY

items.title: Shadowfax delivered exceptional Q4 FY26 results with revenue growth of 73.6% YoY (2.3x management's 25-30% guidance) and PAT turning positive at ₹55....
items.description_html:
Shadowfax delivered exceptional Q4 FY26 results with revenue growth of 73.6% YoY (2.3x management's 25-30% guidance) and PAT turning positive at ₹55.83 Cr from a loss. EBITDA margin expanded 487 bps YoY to 6.6%, exceeding the annual guidance target by 70-100 bps.

The company demonstrated strong operating leverage with costs growing slower than revenue, and generated robust free cash flow of ₹165 Cr. Strategic initiatives including the OneNCR facility and Shadowfax 360 launch position the company well for sustained growth, though working capital needs as D2C业务 scales warrant monitoring.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 73.6% YoY to ₹1,237 Cr in Q4, delivering 2.3x management's 25-30% guidance

PAT turned positive at ₹55.83 Cr vs loss of ₹9.86 Cr YoY, swing of ₹65.69 Cr

EBITDA margin expanded 200 bps FY YoY (3.1% to
5.1%), exceeding ~1-1.2% annual guidance target

Operating cash flow strong at ₹350 Cr, 3.1x PAT; FCF positive at ₹165 Cr vs negative ₹36 Cr FY25

Network scale expanded: 4,519 touchpoints (+32.4% YoY), 2.5L delivery partners (+47.3% YoY)

OneNCR facility operationalized Feb 2026 - 2.1 lakh sq ft, 48,000 shipments/hour capacity

Risk Factors

Working capital absorbed ₹186.66 Cr in receivables growth vs ₹164.81 Cr payables, potential cash flow pressure as D2C scales

Subsidiary (Criticalog India) creates PAT drag of ₹3.47 Cr despite 2.9% revenue contribution

Depreciation grew 80% YoY vs 69% revenue growth - front-loaded capex creating near-term pressure

Q4 operational KPIs not disclosed in quarterly results - limited visibility on quarterly trends

items.title: HUDCO delivered strong operational performance with 29.1% loan book growth and best-in-class asset quality (GNPA 1.04%, NNPA 0.05%). However, reported...
items.description_html:
HUDCO delivered strong operational performance with 29.1% loan book growth and best-in-class asset quality (GNPA 1.04%, NNPA 0.05%). However, reported PAT growth of 48.9% is inflated by a one-time ₹1,459 crore DTL reversal - normalized PAT actually declined 4.9% YoY. NIM missed guidance by 8-18 bps and FCNR losses persisted at ₹936 crore.

The loan book target and disbursement targets were met, but core profitability faces pressure from rising finance costs. NBFC-IFC regulatory compliance remains outstanding.

Positive Factors

Loan book grew 29.1% YoY to ₹1,60,547.15 crore, exceeding ₹1.6 Lakh Cr target by ₹547 crore

Disbursements reached ₹51,194 crore at upper end of guidance, growing 27.9% YoY

GNPA improved 63 bps YoY to 1.04%, NNPA declined to near-zero 0.05%

Provision Coverage Ratio strengthened to 94.90% from 85.44%

PPOP grew 25.6% YoY to ₹3,912.32 crore with strong operating leverage

Cost-to-income ratio improved 84 bps to 9.90%

Risk Factors

Normalized PAT declined 4.9% YoY to ₹2,575.30 crore after excluding ₹1,459 crore DTL reversal

Q4 normalized PAT declined 38% YoY indicating significant core profitability deterioration

NIM missed guidance by 8-18 bps due to backloaded disbursements and higher finance costs

Finance costs grew 32.4% YoY outpacing income growth of 28.4%

FCNR hedging losses of ₹936.63 crore persisted beyond management's 'last quarter' comment

NBFC-IFC compliance (75% infrastructure exposure) not met, regulatory risk pending

3. Insulator shortage

Industry will grow but in a B2B business, stories are never straight forward.

Next thing to do is to always track the order book of these cos

items.title: LT Foods delivered strong revenue growth (26% YoY) exceeding guidance but missed EBITDA margin target significantly (10.63% vs 12.5-13%). Margin compr...
items.description_html:
LT Foods delivered strong revenue growth (26% YoY) exceeding guidance but missed EBITDA margin target significantly (10.63% vs 12.5-13%). Margin compression driven by 49% surge in finance costs and 32% higher depreciation from capacity expansion.

Cash generation doubled YoY with positive FCF despite heavy capex. The company shows revenue execution strength but profitability pressure from debt-funded expansion and new business integration.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 26.07% YoY to ₹10,946 crore, exceeding double-digit growth guidance

Q4 revenue grew 30.44% YoY showing sustained momentum

Operating cash flow doubled YoY to ₹910.5 crore with OCF/PAT at 1.46x

Free cash flow positive at ₹550.9 crore despite ₹360 crore capex

Credit rating upgraded to CRISIL AA/Stable from AA-/Positive

US tariff impact managed - pricing power evident with 30% Q4 growth despite customer resistance

Risk Factors

EBITDA margin missed guidance at 10.63% vs 12.5-13% target (187-237 bps gap)

Finance costs surged 49% YoY (₹13,086 lakh) driven by capex and Golden Star acquisition

Depreciation rose 32% YoY (₹24,489 lakh) from new facilities and intangibles

Normalized PAT grew only 1.12% YoY despite 26% revenue growth - core profitability barely grew

Associates/JV profit down 79% YoY (₹593 lakh vs ₹2,862 lakh)

Borrowings increased 31% YoY to ₹970 crore, continuing debt buildup

1) Go after currency speculators
2) Go after Gold

Options to bring in dollars seem focused:

Real problems - oil (can’t do much except raise prices but no action) and equities (no mention of any relief for them).

So how are equity investors feeling about this ?

items.title: JSW Steel delivered a transformational Q4 FY26 with record quarterly revenue of ₹51,180 cr (14.2% YoY) and EBITDA margin expansion of 460 bps QoQ to ...
items.description_html:
JSW Steel delivered a transformational Q4 FY26 with record quarterly revenue of ₹51,180 cr (14.2% YoY) and EBITDA margin expansion of 460 bps QoQ to 19.0%, driven by steel price recovery and operating leverage.

The BPSL slump sale generated an exceptional gain of ₹18,051 cr, enabling massive deleveraging (Net Debt/EBITDA to 1.81x from 3.75x cap) and strong FCF generation of ₹10,498 cr. FY27 guidance signals aggressive capex of ₹22,000-24,000 cr for capacity expansion, though near-term production targets are conservative excluding BPSL contribution.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 14.2% YoY to ₹51,180 cr (highest quarterly revenue in company history)

Saleable steel sales reached record 7.97 MT (highest ever), up 6% YoY

Adjusted EBITDA margin expanded 460 bps QoQ to 19.0% from 14.4%

Net debt reduced by ₹26,477 cr to ₹53,870 cr post-BPSL slump sale

EBITDA per tonne improved 42% YoY to ₹12,264/ton

Operating cash flow of ₹25,152 cr achieved 0.99x cash conversion ratio

Debt/Equity improved to 0.91x from 1.17x (FY25)

Risk Factors

Normalized PAT (excluding exceptional items) was ₹3,859 cr vs reported ₹19,243 cr - underlying earnings much lower than headline

BPSL exceptional gain of ₹18,051 cr is one-time and distorts true operating performance

Other expenses grew 20.7% YoY faster than revenue (14.2%), indicating cost pressure

Working capital deployment increased with inventory change of ₹4,973 cr vs ₹551 cr (Q4 FY25)

BREAKING: A newly-released OGE Form 278-T discloses that President Trump filed 3,642 trades involving stocks of public companies between January 1st and March 31st.

Our team has sorted and analyzed the thousands of transactions.

Transactions include hundreds of stocks and ETFs such as Nvidia, Microsoft, Broadcom, Amazon, Apple, Alphabet, Meta, Goldman Sachs, AMD, Airbnb, Palantir, Netflix, Costco, Walmart, JP Morgan, DoorDash, and others.

Individual purchases of Nvidia, Microsoft, Broadcom, Amazon, Apple, and others ranged from $1 million to $5 million in disclosed value.

Individual purchases of AMD, Goldman Sachs, Alphabet, Airbnb, DoorDash, Micron, Stryker, and others ranged from $500,000 to $1 million in disclosed value.

President Trump also reported hundreds of stock sales ranging from $15,000 to up to $25 million.

Should we produce a detailed breakdown of President Trump’s stock purchases and sales?

More details to come.

items.title: PATELENG delivered in-line FY26 results with revenue of ₹5,103 Cr beating guidance by 2% and EBITDA margin of 13.40% within the 13-14% target. Strong...
items.description_html:
PATELENG delivered in-line FY26 results with revenue of ₹5,103 Cr beating guidance by 2% and EBITDA margin of 13.40% within the 13-14% target. Strong balance sheet transformation with 27% debt reduction and 208% cash conversion provides financial flexibility.

However, Q4 showed weakness with 11.8% YoY revenue decline and material cost inflation (42% YoY) pressuring margins. FY27 guidance of 10% revenue growth appears achievable given current order pipeline though order book decline needs reversal.

Positive Factors

Revenue exceeded guidance: ₹5,103 Cr achieved vs ₹5,000 Cr target (+2.06%)

EBITDA margin of 13.40% within management's 13-14% guided range

PAT to owners grew 21.6% YoY to ₹2,945 Mn

Gross debt reduced 27% YoY (₹16,643 Mn to ₹12,161 Mn) post rights issue

Operating cash flow strong at 208% of PAT (₹5,601 Mn)

Net worth grew 17.6% YoY to ₹44,420 Mn

International revenue grew 14.6% YoY to ₹2,484 Mn

Risk Factors

Q4 revenue declined 11.8% YoY to ₹14,215 Mn

Material costs surged 42.2% YoY while revenue grew only 0.2%

Real Estate segment loss of ₹365 Mn vs profit of ₹387 Mn in FY25

Order book declining trend (₹16,285 Cr Q1 → ₹15,123 Cr Q3)

Q4 PAT declined 65.8% QoQ due to exceptional charges

items.title: RT Kushal Lodha: Had an amazing time at @NSEIndia meeting MD & CEO, @ashishchauhan and having a tour of the entire building. What stood out was the tr...
items.description_html:
RT Kushal Lodha
Had an amazing time at @NSEIndia meeting MD & CEO, @ashishchauhan and having a tour of the entire building.

What stood out was the trading floor, and the amazing cyber security system that NSE has in order to ensure there are no cyber scams that happen.

It was always a dream to ring the bell at NSE, although it'll be even better if it is by listing my own company haha, but at least the manifestation begins now.

What an amazing day, surrounded with the best content creators in world of finance and business, and meeting them after so long. It is always a pleasure to learn from creators in your category. I highly recommend everyone to form a group of 3-5 content creators in your similar category and of your size so you guys can brainstorm every week / fortnight about content.

Thank you so much to @PurpleBlueHouse and @PreritaC for helping us organise this and making this happen. Also, thank you so much for the wonderful dinner at the Intercontinental Hotel with the marvellous sunset view!

Looking forward to more of such opportunities in the future.

Highly grateful!

#NSE #finance #motivation #inspiration

items.title: RT ValueEquity: Q4FY26 pessimism rate of change tracker, my thoughts: 1)Microfinance : Clear signs of recovery across the board as we had expected as ...
items.description_html:
RT ValueEquity
Q4FY26 pessimism rate of change tracker, my thoughts:

1)Microfinance : Clear signs of recovery across the board as we had expected as well here , improvement in asset quality and loan growth is visible , players like Northern arc have already shown strong numbers with good visibility as well compared to last year , thing to note here is that it will not be as linear as the last cycle , as we are seeing from sector wide commentaries that there is a problem with the JLG mode of business , and Armaan one of the best players in this space has been pointing this out for a few quarters now , I think the leaders will be the ones that can keep coming up with newer and more innovative products for their asset side to keep growing well , also market will now pay a geography multiple as well , we have seen state governments get increasingly hostile with what happened in Karnataka last year , and hence geographical diversification as well as the quality newer products will be the factors to watch , Northern Arc and Armaan seem to be ahead in this space , players like northern arc are far more enabled for sustainable growth that is data driven and cross cyclical

2) Chemicals : Things are starting to take shape in this sector in the commodity chemicals side of it as we had already turned positive in Q3 and overweight 2 weeks back , there are multiple factors leading to this from the Middle eastern conflicts causing the first layer of inflationary price hikes and improvements in the spreads , added by the Chinese anti involution policy that is helping on the rationalization of the overall pricing environment for the commodities, on the Nitration front as well because of the large fire incident in March in China there will now be much tighter regulations and that may affect some smaller players again leading to some price inflation in some chemicals

Yes there have been some RM shortages also as highlighted by the companies but again the larger players are much more equipped to keep dealing with this

Amines duo's , Alkyl has commented that there is clear improvement in the spreads and they are not seeing prices cool off to the level before the conflict any time soon , on their methyl's portfolio there is some competition because after many years aarti has set up a plant here , but that does not exist in the ethyl's side , which is still largely a 2 player market in size

As of now this is more of a supply side price situation but this is helping industry wide spreads ,Alkyl , Styrenix performance and Aarti will be beneficiaries in what I like

3) MDF/Plywood /Laminates - there are clear signs of market structure improving in early stages and the larger players benefiting , Greenply has spoken about a successful 15% price hikes in MDF and 4-5% price hikes in the Plywood segment , and there is a lot of stress in the smaller players in this space because of very high RM costs leading to very tight terms of supply , quoting the management " some suppliers are blackmailing smaller players" and " many smaller players are in a hand to mouth " situation , this is clearly indicating market share gains for larger players and inflationary gains helping them post a capex cycle , they have also invested into better Plywood technology that should help them increase the quality which will compare with high quality Asian peers

Clear indications of : Market share gains + Better pricing , hence a larger pool of profits

Greenlam has just ended a large capex cycle and they should also see their TAM expand substantially and then according to channel checks some improved pricing , and hence could also see better times ahead if the demand also starts recovering on this front

4) PVC pipes - From speaking to friends and associates doing business in the Industry , there has been an absolute rollercoaster ride in the resin prices starting Feb -march , first doubling then correcting severely and then stabilizing finally , things seem to be improving here as well as we see players like supreme focusing aggressively on market share. Apollo pipes has also turned aggressive now targeting 30% growth with Sanjay Gupta , the main man from APL Apollo steel tubes business coming here the management tone has completely changed , their strategy to growth has gotten clear with Kisan , taking their realizations higher as well and increasing focus on leveraging the dealer network of APL apollo tubes into the pipes so they can engage higher volumes and focus on increasing the market share ( disc: we are invested in Apollo pipes from 273 , and it has done well quickly , no reco ) , but now the question is on how sustainable this remains considering the supply structure and the expectations of government spending coming back as a election cycle has just ended , but can say with some confidence that the worst is over for this space, focus now now on the better players ( valuation/growth adjusted)

Now the new addition which is IT

5) The IT space has seen massive disruption , I was speaking to the fund manager at one of India's largest Family offices recently and he also termed it as a "de rating " event for the sector which will still have companies surviving , but it is early to say who will gain from it yet , which I kind of agree with , but I think we must start reading and focusing on the companies that are enabling Agentic AI solutions at enterprise level and already at the forefront of delivering such enterprise deployable efficiency based solutions to their existing client pools

I see companies like Indegene , Coforge , Intellect and few others at the forefront of being agile and prepared for the next levers of growth because they are already enabling the AI integrated offerings in which billables are more outcome based and not the typical hours based

On AI code , to quote the management of Coforge

" The deflationary argument assumes code generation is the entire job. It is not. AI-generated code is cheap to build, but it is expensive to own. It is expensive to secure and it is expensive to maintain. Just like cloud migration, agentic , AI will create a massive managed services layer. And once these systems are in
production, someone has to monitor the models, retrain the agents, and ensure governance. That is a recurring, high-margin revenue stream for firms that can seize it"

So companies that can create this AI first layer that integrates with agility and can also create cost and margin benefits in the future to the enterprise AI players will take a large part of the profit pool home , which is where opportunities for us will lie and we must be prepared to take those

That's it for this tracker folks , if you found some value in this do share it with someone who may be interested , and comment if you did

@ishmohit1

disc: none of the securities mentioned above are investment advices or financial reco's, I may be biased in few of the names mentioned as this is just my opinion from public information

items.title: Q4FY26 pessimism rate of change tracker, my thoughts: 1)Microfinance : Clear signs of recovery across the board as we had expected as well here , impr...
items.description_html:
Q4FY26 pessimism rate of change tracker, my thoughts:

1)Microfinance : Clear signs of recovery across the board as we had expected as well here , improvement in asset quality and loan growth is visible , players like Northern arc have already shown strong numbers with good visibility as well compared to last year , thing to note here is that it will not be as linear as the last cycle , as we are seeing from sector wide commentaries that there is a problem with the JLG mode of business , and Armaan one of the best players in this space has been pointing this out for a few quarters now , I think the leaders will be the ones that can keep coming up with newer and more innovative products for their asset side to keep growing well , also market will now pay a geography multiple as well , we have seen state governments get increasingly hostile with what happened in Karnataka last year , and hence geographical diversification as well as the quality newer products will be the factors to watch , Northern Arc and Armaan seem to be ahead in this space , players like northern arc are far more enabled for sustainable growth that is data driven and cross cyclical

2) Chemicals : Things are starting to take shape in this sector in the commodity chemicals side of it as we had already turned positive in Q3 and overweight 2 weeks back , there are multiple factors leading to this from the Middle eastern conflicts causing the first layer of inflationary price hikes and improvements in the spreads , added by the Chinese anti involution policy that is helping on the rationalization of the overall pricing environment for the commodities, on the Nitration front as well because of the large fire incident in March in China there will now be much tighter regulations and that may affect some smaller players again leading to some price inflation in some chemicals

Yes there have been some RM shortages also as highlighted by the companies but again the larger players are much more equipped to keep dealing with this

Amines duo's , Alkyl has commented that there is clear improvement in the spreads and they are not seeing prices cool off to the level before the conflict any time soon , on their methyl's portfolio there is some competition because after many years aarti has set up a plant here , but that does not exist in the ethyl's side , which is still largely a 2 player market in size

As of now this is more of a supply side price situation but this is helping industry wide spreads ,Alkyl , Styrenix performance and Aarti will be beneficiaries in what I like

3) MDF/Plywood /Laminates - there are clear signs of market structure improving in early stages and the larger players benefiting , Greenply has spoken about a successful 15% price hikes in MDF and 4-5% price hikes in the Plywood segment , and there is a lot of stress in the smaller players in this space because of very high RM costs leading to very tight terms of supply , quoting the management " some suppliers are blackmailing smaller players" and " many smaller players are in a hand to mouth " situation , this is clearly indicating market share gains for larger players and inflationary gains helping them post a capex cycle , they have also invested into better Plywood technology that should help them increase the quality which will compare with high quality Asian peers

Clear indications of : Market share gains + Better pricing , hence a larger pool of profits

Greenlam has just ended a large capex cycle and they should also see their TAM expand substantially and then according to channel checks some improved pricing , and hence could also see better times ahead if the demand also starts recovering on this front

items.title: Prism Johnson reported FY26 with operating margin expansion of 274 bps and segment EBIT growth of 920%, driven by cement segment turnaround and cost d...
items.description_html:
Prism Johnson reported FY26 with operating margin expansion of 274 bps and segment EBIT growth of 920%, driven by cement segment turnaround and cost discipline. However, Q4 cement margin compression to 0.62% and overall PAT decline of 8.8% due to RQBE discontinuation losses temper optimism.

The ₹324 Cr RQBE divestment and balance sheet strengthening (current ratio above 1x) are positive catalysts, but competitive pricing pressure in cement and ongoing RMC losses remain concerns.

Positive Factors

FY26 operating margin expanded 274 bps YoY to 9.46% from 6.72% in FY25

Segment EBIT grew 920% YoY from ₹18.45 Cr to ₹188.24 Cr

Cement segment turned profitable at ₹149.54 Cr vs loss ₹3.38 Cr in FY25

Finance costs declined 20.6% YoY to ₹176.65 Cr

Current ratio improved to 1.09x from 0.84x in FY25

Interest coverage improved to 6.03x from 4.03x in FY25

RQBE divestment approved for ₹324 Cr, eliminating insurance losses

Risk Factors

Cement Q4 margin compressed to 0.62% from 6.19% YoY due to competitive pricing

Total PAT declined 8.8% YoY to ₹72.90 Cr affected by RQBE loss

Working capital outflow of ~₹150 Cr in FY26

RMC segment still loss-making at ₹19.10 Cr (improved from ₹28.50 Cr)

FY26 normalized PAT negative at ₹7.50 Cr after removing exceptional gains

Free cash flow declined 58.4% YoY to ₹144.88 Cr

My latest EAC-PM working paper: "How to do Process Reform: Case Study of IEPFA" (co-authored with @AakankshaArora5). The paper lays out step-by-step how process reforms were implemented at IEFPA over the last one year. As a result, the system is now able to do more approvals in a month than it was earlier doing in a year. Indeed, the backlog will be fully cleared in the next six months. This case study is part of our effort to document government re-engineering efforts so that they can be taught and replicated across the system.

items.title: SENORES delivered strong revenue growth of 53.4% YoY in Q4 and 58.8% for FY26, significantly beating the 25%+ guidance target. The ANDA portfolio doub...
items.description_html:
SENORES delivered strong revenue growth of 53.4% YoY in Q4 and 58.8% for FY26, significantly beating the 25%+ guidance target. The ANDA portfolio doubled to 51 approvals with 30+ products awaiting commercialization, providing strong pipeline visibility.

However, EBITDA margin of 26.6% missed the 30% target by ~340 bps, and working capital stress is evident with receivables growing 162% YoY. The company is in a heavy investment phase with negative FCF of -₹202.68 Cr, though this supports future growth from capacity expansion and acquisitions.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 58.8% YoY to ₹632.63 Cr, beating 25%+ growth target

PAT grew 108.3% YoY to ₹121.53 Cr

EPS grew 63.7% YoY to ₹26.39

ANDA approvals doubled from 26 to 51 in FY26

30+ approved ANDAs yet to be commercialized

Emerging Markets business is now cash flow positive

Regulated Markets revenue grew 169% YoY in Q4

Operating leverage positive: expenses grew 38.1% vs revenue 53.4%

Risk Factors

EBITDA margin 26.6% missed 30% target by ~340 bps
Trade receivables grew 162% YoY, outpacing revenue

Working capital intensity: CFO/PAT ratio only 51.1%

Free cash flow negative at -₹202.68 Cr due to ₹264.73 Cr capex

Regulated Markets revenue ₹224.3 Cr missed ₹400 Cr target

Current ratio declined from 3.01x to 1.91x

Cash balance declined from ₹105.38 Cr to ₹45.55 Cr

items.title: IRFC delivered mixed results with strong execution on sanctions (₹72,949 crore, +22% vs target) and disbursements (₹35,067 crore, +17% vs target), v...
items.description_html:
IRFC delivered mixed results with strong execution on sanctions (₹72,949 crore, +22% vs target) and disbursements (₹35,067 crore, +17% vs target), validating its diversification strategy. NIM expanded 10 bps to 1.50% and margin improved significantly as interest income now represents 35% of revenue. However, PAT growth of 7.80% YoY missed the double-digit target, and Q4 PAT was nearly flat at 0.15% YoY.

The massive lease receivable recognition (₹1.65 lakh crore) drove operating cash flow negative but represents future income streams. FY27 outlook supported by record ₹70,000 crore borrowing programme and pending lease agreements.

Positive Factors

Interest income grew 47.31% YoY in Q4 and 23.58% FY, driving diversification into higher-margin financing

Sanctions of ₹72,949 crore exceeded target by 22%, disbursements of ₹35,067 crore exceeded target by 17%

NIM expanded 10 bps to 1.50%, validating diversification strategy into higher-margin assets

Operating profit margin expanded 176 bps YoY to 25.69%, net profit margin expanded 171 bps to 25.65%

Total assets crossed ₹5 lakh crore milestone for the first time in IRFC's history

Zero NPA maintained despite book expansion, asset quality remains pristine

Finance costs effectively managed at 4.59% despite larger balance sheet, well below 7% threshold

Risk Factors

PAT growth of 7.80% YoY missed management's double-digit target, Q4 PAT grew only 0.15% YoY

Operating cash flow negative ₹27,026 crore due to ₹1.65 lakh crore lease receivable recognition

Impairment provisions surged to ₹124 crore from ₹0.68 crore due to RBI-mandated Standard Asset Provisioning

Revenue growth FY26 was marginal at 0.49% YoY, Q4 acceleration may not be sustainable

AUM at ₹4.85 lakh crore missed ₹5 lakh crore target, near-miss at 97%

items.title: If one wants to be contrarian in IT after the recent sell-off, the key point is that the market is currently not differentiating much. Correction has ...
items.description_html:
If one wants to be contrarian in IT after the recent sell-off, the key point is that the market is currently not differentiating much. Correction has been across the board.

Some correction was also necessary. It forces companies to realise that the old way of operating may not work anymore. They need to evolve faster, especially with AI.

If someone wants to take IT exposure purely as a contrarian bet, I would still prefer midcap IT over largecap IT.

In Midcap IT, there are a few better choices like -
Persistent Systems, Coforge, LTIMindtree, Mphasis

Among these, considering execution track record, management commentary & future outlook : Persistent Systems & Coforge seems to be better placed.

Their concalls were also worth listening to, as management shared a lot of useful insights on demand, deal wins, AI adoption, vertical wise growth & future outlook.

The interesting part is that price action in IT stocks is now making many of them look more like yield plays than growth stocks, especially in largecap IT.

With valuations correcting & FCF yields moving closer to 7-8%, markets are almost treating them like bonds.

Now coming to ITBEES -

ITBEES can be a decent way to take broad exposure to the IT sector, but it comes with one limitation.

It is heavily tilted towards largecap IT names.

So if the view is simply that the entire IT sector is oversold & should see mean reversion, then ITBEES can work.

But if the view is that midcap IT will continue outperforming largecap IT, then ITBEES may not be the best way to express that view.

Because ITBEES will mainly give exposure to Infosys, TCS, HCL Tech, Tech Mahindra & Wipro, with limited weight to midcap names.

What are your thoughts, mention below⏬


Equity Insights Elite: CC Growth Trend of IT Companies

Coforge & Persistent Systems continue to be outperformers

Midcap >> Largecap.

items.title: CLEAN reported a challenging FY26 with EBITDA declining 8.40% against management's 15-18% growth guidance, missing by over 23 percentage points. Margi...
items.description_html:
CLEAN reported a challenging FY26 with EBITDA declining 8.40% against management's 15-18% growth guidance, missing by over 23 percentage points. Margin compression of 299 bps to 37.11% also fell short of the 40% target. However, Q4 showed sequential recovery with 13.46% QoQ revenue growth and working capital normalized, providing some near-term relief.

The FMCG segment collapse (12% to 7% share) and subsidiary losses remain concerns, though Performance Chemicals' dominance (76% share) with no visible competition offers some strategic positioning.

Positive Factors

Q4 showed sequential recovery with revenue up 13.46% QoQ and EBITDA up 32.65% QoQ

Working capital normalized: net release of ₹116.45 million vs absorption of ₹721.83 million in FY25

Strong cash conversion: CFO/PAT ratio at 1.20x indicating high-quality earnings

Near-zero debt maintained: Debt/Equity at 0.001x providing financial flexibility

Performance Chemicals segment grew to 76% of
revenue with no visible competition in MEHQ/anisole derivatives

Dividend maintained at ₹4 per share with ~28% payout ratio

Risk Factors

EBITDA declined 8.40% YoY vs guidance of 15-18% growth — miss of 23.4-26.4 percentage points

EBITDA margin of 37.11% missed 40% target by 299 bps

FMCG segment collapsed from 12% to 7% of revenue due to customer loss in China

Subsidiaries created 9.39% PAT drag (-₹215.62 million) indicating scale-up losses

HALS capacity utilization only ~25-30% vs target for breakeven

Management credibility impacted by missing all major guidance targets

items.title: Dwarikesh reported strong PAT growth of 32.16% YoY driven by significant tax optimization (ETR down from 55.52% to 27.47%) and exceptional cash genera...
items.description_html:
Dwarikesh reported strong PAT growth of 32.16% YoY driven by significant tax optimization (ETR down from 55.52% to 27.47%) and exceptional cash generation (CFO 4.19x PAT). The company achieved aggressive deleveraging with 33.7% debt reduction and improved liquidity (current ratio 2.33x).

Distillery segment showed exceptional margin expansion (+3,902 bps) offsetting sugar segment weakness. However, revenue declined 7.29% YoY in Q4 and sugar margins remain compressed. Forward guidance targets 340 lakh quintals cane crushing for SS 2026-27.

Positive Factors

PAT grew 32.16% YoY to ₹3,083.80 Lakh despite EBITDA declining 23.43%

Operating cash flow was 4.19x PAT at ₹12,911.95 Lakh indicating exceptional cash quality

Debt reduced by ₹16,950.65 Lakh (33.7%) improving Debt/Equity from 0.62x to 0.40x

Distillery segment margin expanded 3,902 bps YoY to 55.58% in Q4

Free cash flow of ₹12,207.46 Lakh generated in FY26

Working capital released ₹4,916.46 Lakh with inventory reduction of ₹7,282.54 Lakh

Current ratio improved to 2.33x from 1.90x indicating strong liquidity

Risk Factors

Q4 revenue declined 7.29% YoY to ₹42,542.55 Lakh

Sugar segment margin compressed 154 bps FY YoY to 2.41% due to UP SAP cost pressure

EBITDA margin contracted 297 bps YoY to 20.35% in Q4

Distillery revenue declined 30.40% YoY to ₹13,195.62 Lakh in Q4

Full year revenue growth of 3.17% was modest after 20.53% decline in FY25

Red rot disease impacted cane availability affecting sugar production

In the short term, valuations doesn't matter much when momentum is strong

But over the long term, markets do take note of valuations

We have already seen multiple eg where expensive stocks kept moving for some time, but once growth expectations cooled off, the correction was brutal

At high valuations, even a small disappointment in numbers, margins or guidance can lead to sharp derating

Trailing such positions is the better approach

Otherwise, history has shown that the end result in highly expensive stocks is usually not good when expectations become unrealistic


Equity Insights Elite: Lot of discussion around MTAR

Stock is already up 300%

Results are good, but expectations were much higher

Everything aside, current MCap is 20k Cr

This year ended with slightly less than 100 Cr PAT

Even if it does 400-500 Cr PAT, it would still trade at 40-50x

items.title: RT Himanta Biswa Sarma: The Ironman is widely regarded as one of the toughest endurance events in the world. It is a grueling single-day test of stami...
items.description_html:
RT Himanta Biswa Sarma
The Ironman is widely regarded as one of the toughest endurance events in the world. It is a grueling single-day test of stamina and determination. Participants must complete a 3.8 km swim, a 180 km cycling ride, and a full 42.2 km marathon, all back to back.

On 10 May, my son Nandil, currently in his fifth year at the National Law School of India University in Bengaluru, took on this extraordinary challenge at Ironman Vietnam. After just 7-8 months of preparation, he completed the challenge in approximately 16 hours.

As a father, it fills me with immense pride to see his discipline, determination and perseverance. I wish him the very best for the journey ahead and for many more milestones in life.

items.description_html:
RT Chandra R. Srikanth
HCLTech leading Sarvam round is a rare instance of an Indian IT company leading an investment in an AI startup. It also comes at a time when IT companies are struggling to grow.

While HCLTech will invest around $150 million in Sarvam AI, Bessemer Venture Partners (BVP) will put in another $50 million as part of the deal,"

“The remaining $100 million will come jointly from Nvidia, Prosperity7, Activate, Glade Brook and others, including existing backers.

Sarvam AI is backed by investors such as Khosla Ventures, Lightspeed, Peak XV Partners and others which are all expected to participate in the round, per sources

Moneycontrol had exclusively reported on March 24 Sarvam AI had begun talks to raise $250 million from HCLTech, NVIDIA, Accel and others in a fresh funding round which will be among the largest for an Indian AI startup in recent years

A few days later, Moneycontrol was also first to report on April 1 that Accel had passed and Bessemer Venture Partners was actively engaging with the company to close a funding round.

items.title: RT Neelkanth Mishra: With China's significant inventory releases calming physical oil markets (physical premiums are down) but pushing up future oil p...
items.description_html:
RT Neelkanth Mishra
With China's significant inventory releases calming physical oil markets (physical premiums are down) but pushing up future oil prices (Mar-2027 delivery now reaching $85-90), the economy now needs to adjust to sustained higher oil prices.
Of the 2% of GDP terms-of-trade shock, we estimate the government has absorbed around 1.2% so far, and 0.8% has flowed through. As fuel prices at the pump increase, more of the growth headwinds will show up. But the binding constraint is on the external account (the INR), and the economy needs to adjust to energy market realities.
My column today in the Times of India
https://tessellatum.in/?p=619

items.title: Americans are very worried about their jobs: The perceived probability among US consumers that the unemployment rate will be higher in 1-year increase...
items.description_html:
Americans are very worried about their jobs:

The perceived probability among US consumers that the unemployment rate will be higher in 1-year increased +0.4 percentage points in April, to 43.9%, the highest in 12 months.

Excluding April 2025, this is the highest reading since the 2020 pandemic spike, according to the NY Fed.

Since the start of 2025, this percentage has risen +10 points.

Meanwhile, 61% of consumers expect unemployment to increase over the next 12 months, according to the University of Michigan Surveys of Consumers.

By comparison, only 14% of respondents expected unemployment to rise in 2021.

This level of pessimism has never happened outside of a recession.

Job market expectations are at recession-like levels.

items.title: BREAKING: US data center construction spending jumped +34% YoY in March, to a record $50 billion annualized rate. Spending on data centers is up +437%...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: US data center construction spending jumped +34% YoY in March, to a record $50 billion annualized rate.

Spending on data centers is up +437% since the beginning of 2021, when the annualized rate stood at ~$9 billion.

This is also up +688% since the start of 2018, when the annualized rate was just ~$6 billion.

Meanwhile, office building construction spending fell -9% YoY in March, to $46 billion, the lowest since 2015.

This means that spending on data centers now exceeds office building construction by $4 billion, or +9%.

To put this into perspective, office construction spending exceeded data center spending by $65 billion, or +650%, in 2020.

AI is fundamentally transforming the US economy.

Money supply is skyrocketing:

Global money supply is now up to a record $121.9 trillion.

Over the last 2 years, money supply has soared +$17.1 trillion, or +16%.

This also marks a +$27 trillion increase, or +28%, since the 2022 low.

This means that global money supply is surging +7% to +8% a year.

Meanwhile, US M2 money supply jumped +$1 trillion YoY, or +4.6%, to a record $22.7 trillion.

items.title: Risk appetite is skyrocketing: Assets under management (AUM) in US leveraged ETFs are up to a record $177 billion. Since the March bottom, total lever...
items.description_html:
Risk appetite is skyrocketing:

Assets under management (AUM) in US leveraged ETFs are up to a record $177 billion.

Since the March bottom, total leveraged ETF AUM has surged +$45 billion.

Tech-oriented ETFs account for the majority of total AUM, at ~69%

This comes as technology AUM stands at $65 billion, followed by semiconductors at $32 billion, and the Magnificent 7 at $25 billion.

Furthermore, leveraged ETF AUM linked to the S&P 500 is $24 billion.

To put this into perspective, total leveraged ETF AUM was just ~$30 billion in 2020.

Investors are piling into leveraged funds at a record pace.

items.title: RT Hiba Nasr: ‼️Vance in response to @JenniferJJacobs on the state of the negotiations with Iran: I spent a good amount of time on the phone with wi...
items.description_html:
RT Hiba Nasr
‼️Vance in response to @JenniferJJacobs on the state of the negotiations with Iran:

I spent a good amount of time on the phone with with both Jared Kushner and Steve would call this morning a number of our friends. In the Arab world this morning are making progress. The fundamental question is, do we make enough progress that we satisfy the President's red line? And the red line is very simple. We need to feel confident that we put a number of protections in place such that Iran will never have a nuclear weapon. That is the question, do we meet that threshold or not? I think that we've made a lot of progress since we left Pakistan. I thought we made some progress in Pakistan, but we've made more since then. But that is, fundamentally what we're negotiating towards, is we want to be able to look the American people in the eye and say with confidence that you are not going to have to worry about this very, very dangerous regime having access to the most dangerous weapons of the world that's the goal for focus on, again, there's a lot of ways to accomplish that goal, the President has set us off on the diplomatic pathway for now and that's what I'm focused on.

Most investors missed the shift — because they were still studying the previous cycle.

This earnings season, something similar may be happening again.

When you stop focusing on headline “beats” and instead study where the real compounding is happening, a different picture emerges.

items.title: A Masterclass on Cash Flow Statements🚨 Recently reviewed an SME company’s Cash Flow Statement and found some shocking classifications: • Non-curr...
items.description_html:
A Masterclass on Cash Flow Statements🚨

Recently reviewed an SME company’s Cash Flow Statement and found some shocking classifications:

• Non-current liabilities adjusted under working capital changes- even though they belong to Financing Activity/(else depend on line item but not shown as part of Operating Activity)

• Current investments deducted from Operating Cash Flow: turning positive CFO into negative -
Either shown as a part of Investing Activity or as a part of Cash Equivalent

• Only gain/loss on sale of assets shown, but actual sale proceeds missing from Investing Activity

• For the first time ever found interesting line item in Cash Flow from Financing Activity - “Proceeds from issue of Security Premium” 👀(Just Disclosure Change - Proceeds from issue of equity share)

Interestingly How this Man matched the Cash Balance?
Comment below - what can be the possible balancing figure here?

One more Interesting part?
The company raised funds to purchase a corporate office. This is exactly why smart investors don’t stop at Revenue, EBITDA or PAT.

The real story hides inside:
• Cash flow classifications
• Notes to accounts
• Working capital adjustments

#redflags

items.title: WABAG and PEAK announce first Bio-CNG plant in Ghaziabad; commercial operations expected within one year This Project marks the first milestone under ...
items.description_html:
WABAG and PEAK announce first Bio-CNG plant in Ghaziabad; commercial operations expected within one year

This Project marks the first milestone under their waste-to-energy initiative announced on January 29, 2024, with a shared vision to establish 100 Bio-CNG plants at sewage treatment facilities across India and other markets.

The Bio-CNG Plant will be developed at the 70 MLD Sewage Treatment Plant (STP) in Dundaheda, Ghaziabad, under a Build-Operate-Transfer (BOT) model on a Public-Private Partnership basis

The Plant will capture raw biogas generated at the STP, preventing methane emissions into the atmosphere, and upgrade it into high-quality renewable Bio-CNG fuel

WABAG will lead the project execution and undertake operations and maintenance for 15 years.

Good to see WABAG focus on other forms of energy production as well, not just from WATER

items.description_html:
Nuvama Wealth : 5 Yr OP Profit CAGR : 39%

Dividend of 14 per share was declared for H2FY26🔥

Dividend payout continues to remain at 50% of profit

The key highlight is the changing business mix

Q4 REV grew 7% YoY, but excluding IE & IB, REV grew 13%

FY26 ex IE/IB REV grew 17%, while Wealth + Private grew 20%

Wealth + Private now contributes around 55%

This mix shift is important because these businesses are more predictable compared to Institutional Equities & Investment Banking

Asset Services has recovered fully from the Q1 disruption

Management highlighted that asset services is more like a market infrastructure business, not like a pure capital market cyclical business

The mix is shifting towards Wealth, Private Wealth, Asset Management & Asset Services

This improves predictability & reduces cyclicality

items.title: RT Intrinsic Compounding: Shipbuilding revenue follows an S-curve. And every stage of that curve has a different set of winners. 1) Design - Steel & p...
items.description_html:
RT Intrinsic Compounding
Shipbuilding revenue follows an S-curve. And every stage of that curve has a different set of winners.

1) Design - Steel & plates (SAIL, JSW)
2) Block/Hull fabrication - Forgings, castings (Ramkrishna 3) Forgings, Bharat Forge, krishana Defence)
4) Equipment loading (65% of ship cost) - Pumps, valves, engines, cables (KSB, L&T, Paramount, etc.)
5) Outfitting - Electronics, navigation, sensors (Astra, MTAR, Data Patterns, etc. )

NGC orders expected soon for GRSE and revenue kicks in H2 FY28. The real money in FY29+.

Snippet from GRSE concall

Disclaimer - not a recommendation to buy/sell

items.title: RT Bhargavi: India is desperate for stable foreign capital. INR is in a virtual free fall. Yet, there is an *absurd* bottleneck nobody talks about: th...
items.description_html:
RT Bhargavi
India is desperate for stable foreign capital. INR is in a virtual free fall. Yet, there is an *absurd* bottleneck nobody talks about: the RBI’s FIRMS portal — mandatory for FC-GPR filings and therefore for receiving FDI share capital — has been down for weeks.

As a result, inward remittances for legitimate equity investments into Indian companies are getting stuck because founders literally cannot complete regulatory filings.

At a moment when the country needs every dollar of productive FDI it can attract, this is exactly the kind of invisible bureaucratic friction India should not be imposing on itself.

@RBI @RBIsays @NITIAyog @nsitharaman

items.title: RT Persistence Capital: Manufacturing will be a larger part of benchmarks going forward as it's one of the few pockets of the market where growth is b...
items.description_html:
RT Persistence Capital
Manufacturing will be a larger part of benchmarks going forward as it's one of the few pockets of the market where growth is broad-based and secular.

As an example, TCS grew revenues by INR 41,563 cr during FY23-26. During that same period, Dixon grew revenues by INR 36,681 cr. Think about it - Dixon's absolute revenue accretion in the last 3 years was ~88% of the country's largest IT services business even though it's FY23 revenue was ~5% of TCS FY23 revenue.

Nifty 50 has negligible exposure to manufacturing businesses. We doubt that this will still be the case a decade from now.

Disclaimer: No recommendation to buy or sell anything.

items.title: Eternal says their Moat is physical - "Every order on Zomato is a real-world coordination problem. A meal has to be prepared by a restaurant we don't ...
items.description_html:
Eternal says their Moat is physical -

"Every order on Zomato is a real-world coordination problem. A meal has to be prepared by a restaurant we don't control, picked up within minutes by a delivery partner navigating traffic we can't predict, and delivered while the food is still hot. Get any one of those wrong, and the experience breaks.

Blinkit takes this complexity several notches up. We run our own supply chain. 17 million square feet of warehousing and dark store space. Procuring, storing, and managing inventory across thousands of stores, then delivering in minutes. The margin for error is measured in seconds and centimetres of shelf space. District operates in a world where tens of thousands of people converge on a single venue for a few hours, and the experience depends on everything from sound engineering and crowd flows to parking, security, and weather.

But here's the thing about the physical world: it doesn't always cooperate. The weather shuts down roads. Demand spikes without warning. Restaurants run out of ingredients mid-rush. Delivery partners get stuck. And if that complexity weren't enough, we build in India. Our systems interact with the real India - cities where addresses are approximate, road infrastructure is always catching up, supply chains that still run largely on cash and handshake relationships, a largely informal workforce, and a merchant ecosystem that is fragmented in ways that would be unrecognizable to most global tech companies.

This isn't a complaint - it's a description of our moat. Because anyone who figures out how to build reliably in this environment has built something very hard to replicate. Over the last two decades, that's what we've done. We've built systems that don't just work in ideal conditions but hold up under real-world stress.

As a result of this reliability, our platforms have become a part of daily life for over 100 million customers in the country. This trust extends across the ecosystem. Today, our platforms support livelihoods for over 1 million delivery partners, 400k+ restaurants, 100k+ supply chain workers, and thousands of brands. As our scale grows, so does the economic value and impact that we create for our partners."

Src - Deepinder Goyal's Q4FY26 Eternal Shareholder Letter, no reco

items.title: RT Imtiaz Mahmood: I was on a train in Tokyo. We stopped between stations. Announcement in Japanese, then in English: "We apologize for the delay. We ...
items.description_html:
RT Imtiaz Mahmood
I was on a train in Tokyo. We stopped between stations. Announcement in Japanese, then in English: "We apologize for the delay. We will resume shortly."

The delay was maybe 3 minutes. Not a big deal.

When the train started moving again, another announcement: "We sincerely apologize for the delay. We were stopped for 3 minutes and 20 seconds. This is unacceptable. Thank you for your patience."

Three minutes and twenty seconds. They measured it exactly. And called it unacceptable.

When I got off at my stop, there were station staff on the platform bowing and handing out delay certificates.

I took one out of curiosity. It was an official document stating that the train had been delayed by 3 minutes and 20 seconds, signed and stamped.

The staff member said in English "for your employer. So they know the delay was not your fault."

I said I'm a tourist, I don't need it. He looked confused. "But the delay affected you. You deserve an apology."

Three minutes. They were treating a three-minute delay like a major incident.

Later I mentioned this to a Japanese friend. They said "oh yes, delay certificates are normal. Trains are supposed to be exactly on time. If they are late, they must apologize."

I said three minutes isn't late, it's nothing. My friend said "in Japan, three minutes is late. On time means on time. Not approximately on time."

They said the train company probably investigated why there was a 3-minute delay. "They will find the cause and fix it so it doesn't happen again."

I kept the certificate. It's framed in my apartment now. A reminder that somewhere in the world, people care about three minutes.

© 6IX.

@BSAT_Properties

items.title: A lot of defense to IT SIs right now is based on assumption that enterprise clients will not open their systems, data & processes to external AI solut...
items.description_html: A lot of defense to IT SIs right now is based on assumption that enterprise clients will not open their systems, data & processes to external AI solutions (Claude, OpenAi). However, this defense collapses the moment we have a precedence from a big enterprise opening up just the way enterprises opened up to cloud after a decade long friction. The moment this starts happening, the JVs OpenAi & Claude have formed will make a killing. And, what will take this wall down? For e.g., enterprises are currently very upset after SAP API policy that prohibited clients from using their own data & sending it to external tools via APIs to architect a solution outside of SAP. This can be the 1st hammer in the wall. One wrong step & clients will find ways to disconnect with enterprise OEMs & will be willing to trust(with an agreement perhaps) likes of Claude & OpenAi. The moment this happens, it is done!! This is the biggest risk I foresee.

items.title: RT Maheshwer Peri: It isnt surprising that NEET exam has been cancelled by the @NTA_Exams . The hub of this entire operation is Sikar, where the succe...
items.description_html:
RT Maheshwer Peri
It isnt surprising that NEET exam has been cancelled by the @NTA_Exams . The hub of this entire operation is Sikar, where the success rate is 6X of national average. A similar thing happened in 2024 too, but the allegations were brushed under the carpet. Had we dealt with it in 2024 with an iron hand, this wouldnt have repeated.

What we know is that 140 of the 180 questions in NEET were part of a guess paper of 410 questions. What is alarming is that the order of the questions and the options were exactly the same.

The modus operandi is simple. In Sikar, the students are called in for a Mock test, a day before the actual exam and made to prepare for each of the questions in the guess paper. The students had 140 of the 180 questions preepared, thus guaranteed 600 of the 720 marks even before they entered the exam hall.

Time to go after the paper leakers. Jail them. 23 Lac students and families will pay the price of the brushing under the carpet we did in 2024. @careers360

items.title: Deepinder Goyal Q4FY26 Eternal Shareholder Letter – In 2008, I was collecting restaurant menus on a motorbike in Delhi, scanning them, and uploading ...
items.description_html:
Deepinder Goyal Q4FY26 Eternal Shareholder Letter –

In 2008, I was collecting restaurant menus on a motorbike in Delhi, scanning them, and uploading them to a website. I wasn't building a business. I was merely solving a problem I knew others had, in the spirit of service. I never intended it to become a business.

Zomato, the company, was started two years later in 2010, when I started getting advertiser interest on the website, which used to get a little over 5,000 visitors a day.

Over the years, one thing led to another. Last year (FY26), 109 million Indians completed transactions worth over $10 billion through Blinkit, District, and Zomato. The company I accidentally started now touches how India eats, shops, and goes out. But it took us 18 years to get to $10 billion (per year).

This doubling to $20 billion annual NOV will take less than two years from here

Similarly, it took 16 years to reach Adjusted EBITDA profitability in FY24. From there, we expect to reach $1 billion of Adjusted EBITDA, hopefully by FY29

No reco

items.title: RT Rajeev Mantri: 1/ Owing to high barriers to entry, India’s lodging and hotels industry has become a cartel that raises prices at will and fleeces ...
items.description_html:
RT Rajeev Mantri
1/ Owing to high barriers to entry, India’s lodging and hotels industry has become a cartel that raises prices at will and fleeces customers.

India’s luxury hotel average daily rate (ADR) in Mumbai and Delhi now rival those of New York and London. Absurdly enough, the financial press sometimes celebrates this as some triumph of Indian hospitality. It is nothing of the sort. High prices are the unmistakable stamp of a supply-constrained market in a low income country, which should actually have a very competitively priced and deep pool of lodging options. The hotel industry is a structural failure being hyped as a success.

items.title: Higher education is seen as a key to getting young Indians into skilled, high-paying jobs and accelerating economic growth. Just under 40 million youn...
items.description_html:
Higher education is seen as a key to getting young Indians into skilled, high-paying jobs and accelerating economic growth.

Just under 40 million young people (aged 18-23) were enrolled in higher education programmes in India as of 2022. This included both undergraduate (or equivalent) and postgraduate (or equivalent) levels of study.

However, at 32%, enrolment in tertiary or higher education in India is still below the world average.

In the early 1990s, India’s higher education enrolment rates were similar to China’s, for instance. But in the two decades since, China has made big strides: more than seven in ten young Chinese adults are now in higher education, compared to three in ten young Indian adults.

How does higher education enrolment look across states, and what disciplines do students choose to study in India? Read @akwaghmare’s piece to find out: https://www.dataforindia.com/higher-education/?utm_source=twitter&utm_medium=post

#College #Undergraduate #Education #HigherEducation #India #DataForIndia

• EBITDA Margin: 8.45%
↳ Improved by 106 bps QoQ and 416 bps YoY

• PAT: ₹682.43 lakhs
↳ Up 34.04% QoQ and 1,180.60% YoY

FY26 vs FY25
• Revenue: ₹61,799.18 lakhs vs ₹61,575.38 lakhs — 0.36% growth
• EBITDA: ₹4,616.13 lakhs vs ₹3,630.01 lakhs — 27.17% growth
• EBITDA Margin: 7.47% vs 5.90% — up 157 bps
• PAT: ₹1,545.13 lakhs vs ₹914.63 lakhs — 68.93% growth

✅ Key takeaway:
Revenue remained largely stable on a full-year basis, but EBITDA and PAT grew, showing better operating efficiency, improved margins and stronger earnings.

items.title: Very Unique disclosure by recently listed Sedemac To help investors make better-informed buy/hold/sell decisions, the company has instituted the follo...
items.description_html:
Very Unique disclosure by recently listed Sedemac

To help investors make better-informed buy/hold/sell decisions, the company has instituted the following framework. A once-a-year survey is conducted, seeking opinions of select institutional investors and analysts — deemed to have a mature understanding of the business — on the degree of risk across key identified risks. This is followed by a management commentary on select risks during the May investor/earnings call.

This year's survey was conducted between May 1–8, 2026. Invitations were extended to CIOs, Fund Managers, and Analysts across 20 buy-side funds and 3 sell-side analysts. Of the 20 buy-side funds, 10 were DIIs (including 5 of the top 6 mutual funds by AUM), 5 were FIIs, and 5 were pre-IPO investors with significant current holdings. To preserve confidentiality, the survey was conducted anonymously by default, with an option for participants to disclose their identity.

Participants assessed 13 business risks, rating each as Low / Medium / High / No View, based on both the probability of the risk materializing and the severity of its potential impact.

They intend to comment on the highlighted risks in our investor/earnings call likely to held in May 2026.

no reco strictly

items.title: RT Nimesh Shah: Macquarie on SBI O-P, TP Rs 1150 4Q FY26: Earnings miss driven by weak margins and trading losses NIM impacted by higher proportion of...
items.description_html:
RT Nimesh Shah
Macquarie on SBI
O-P, TP Rs 1150
4Q FY26: Earnings miss driven by weak margins and trading losses
NIM impacted by higher proportion of floating book
Asset quality remains a bright spot
Stock trades at 1.6x FY27E P/BV, which appears comfortable given expected ROEs of 15%.

JPM on SBI
OW, TP 1225
SBI reported a weaker than expected 4Q with NII (+3.8% y/y, -2% q/q) at Rs 443.8bn moderating vs +9% y/y in 3Q26 and coming 6% below JPM.
SBI recorded a 17bps q/q decline in NIM (whole bank) to 2.81% and an 18bps q/q decline in NIM (domestic) to 2.93%, vs. Street expectations of flattish to marginally lower margins q/q. However, SBI remains confident of NIM recovery in FY27 vs 4Q backed by multiple steps highlighted below.
Asset quality was benign with credit costs (on avg loans) at 24bps (-16bps q/q), although slippages (+3bps q/q) edged up slightly on seasonality.

MS on SBI
EW, TP Rs 980
Focus on call was on sharp NIM decline.
Management expects some recovery in NIM from 4Q level driven largely by loan yield recovery
Assume some recovery, yet cut F27- 28 NIM forecast by >20bp
Trim F27 & F28 EPS forecasts by 4% & 2%
Management guided to FY27 loan growth of 13-15%, assuming no material macro deterioration

CLSA on SBI
O-P TP Rs 1275
Reported a 4% beat on its 4QFY26 PBT driven by lower-than-expected opex and credit costs & a higher recovery from written-off accounts.
However, on top line, it was a weak quarter, with NII missing estimates by 5%.
This was driven by a 17bp sequential compression in NIM, sharper than expected
Calculated loan yields dropped 40bp to 7.5% driven by repo rate cuts, MCLR repricing & an increase in EBLR share.
Apart from NIM, it was a good quarter.
Loan growth of 17% YoY was best in class, while deposit growth picked up from 9% to 11% YoY
CASA ratio was steady YoY at 39%.
Slippages were steady at 0.5%, & credit costs were 0.2%.

BoFA Sec on SBI
Buy, TP Rs 1200
PAT growth of 6% YoY led by strong loan growth and lower credit cost offset by NIM compression and lower non‑interest income
NIM contraction led by repo‑linked loan repricing, MCLR resets post rate cuts, and mix shift towards T‑bill‑linked loans
FY27 guidance - 13-15% loan growth, full year NIM >3% , <50 bps credit cost and 1%+ RoA

Bernstein on SBI
Market Perform, TP Rs 1300
SBI delivered a mixed 4Q26
Loan growth accelerated to 17% YoY but NIM saw a sharp 18 bps QoQ decline to 2.93%, steepest compression amongst large banks.
Slippages rose sequentially, though largely seasonal, while headline GNPA improved to 1.49%.
RoA moderated 12 bps QoQ to 1.07%, weighed down by NIM compression & a Treasury MTM loss.
Management retained FY27 guidance of 13–15% credit growth, >3% domestic NIM & 50 bps credit costs
Management reaffirmed its through-cycle commitment to 1%+ RoA & 15%+ RoE

UBS on SBI
Neutral, TP Rs 1080
NII declined QoQ as margins dip 18bp QoQ due to loan pricing cut
Loan growth at c5% QoQ led by Corporates; asset quality stable
Management guides for loan growth of 13-15% for FY27; credit cost of c50bp
Expect bank's ROA to see downward pressure as treasury gains and credit costs normalize while margin trends remain subdued
Thus estimate ROA of 0.95% over FY27-28E (vs c1.12% in FY26) & believe risk reward at current valuations (1.2x FY27E P/BV) seems fair
Kotak Inst Eqt on SBI

Buy, TP Rs 1250
SBI reported about 5% yoy earnings growth, led by a 55% yoy decline in provisions, while operating profits declined about 10% yoy
Bank reported solid loan growth (~17% yoy), low credit costs (~25 bps) and a NIM decline of 20 bps qoq
Miss against expectations is likely to prompt a near-term pause in its re-rating, with investors awaiting evidence of recovery before rerating bank.
@CNBCTV18News @_RituSingh

items.title: RT Anthony DiGiorgio, DO, MHA: This is the ultimate midwit healthcare take. No, 32 countries have not “figured out” universal healthcare. The UK has...
items.description_html:
RT Anthony DiGiorgio, DO, MHA
This is the ultimate midwit healthcare take.

No, 32 countries have not “figured out” universal healthcare.

The UK has “free” healthcare, and roughly 1 in 3 cancer patients in England still fail to start treatment within 62 days of urgent referral.

Canada has “free” healthcare, and the median wait for neurosurgical treatment is around a year.

Australia has “free” healthcare, and over half the country still buys private insurance despite paying for a public universal system with their taxes.

Switzerland has universal coverage, because residents are required to buy private insurance. There is no government system where benevolent bureaucrats tuck you in at night with a warm blanket and an MRI appointment.

The actual lesson from other wealthy countries is not “they figured it out.”

America’s system has huge problems. Our prices are insane, insurance markets are distorted, and hospital systems are cartelized. Our regulations make care more expensive than it needs to be.

Yet we still guarantee access to even the 8% who don’t have coverage. We give easy routes to qualify for medicaid for those with disabilities.

Pretending the rest of the world solved healthcare because they slapped the word “universal” on a rationing scheme is not analysis.

It is bumper sticker policy for people who think access means having a card in your wallet while you wait a year to see the doctor you need.


daz: Free Universal Healthcare is so complicated and expensive that only 32 of the 33 wealthiest countries in the world have figured it out.

items.title: Don’t chase commodity chemicals after they’ve already run up significantly from recent lows. Think in terms of probabilities, not binaries. Avoid tr...
items.description_html:
Don’t chase commodity chemicals after they’ve already run up significantly from recent lows. Think in terms of probabilities, not binaries. Avoid trying to predict the next big move. If you own them from lower levels, feel free to hold, provided your thesis was never about just 2 months.

Several detailed presentations covered commodity chemicals ranging from NOCIL to DMCC and Deepak Nitrite. There are 30 other commodity chemical stocks, but the same framework applies to all.

Heuristics work when rules fail. Point #11 is what I seriously follow. Sold Indoco Remedies yesterday at day's high as it failed this test:

1. Think in probabilities instead of making absolute predictions about commodity stocks.
2. Do build positions gradually rather than chasing a stock after a sharp 40-50% rally.
3. Do continuously update your view as new supply, demand, and geopolitical information emerges.
4. Do focus on whether the market has already priced in the bullish narrative before buying. Predictions usually fail, so have a margin of safety!
5. Do preserve capital and wait patiently if the risk-reward becomes unfavourable after a fast/euphoric move.
6. Don’t chase vertically rising stocks out of fear of missing out.
7. Don’t extrapolate recent gains indefinitely into the future, because commodity cycles often reverse sharply.
8. Don’t confuse a compelling geopolitical story with a guaranteed investment outcome.
9. Don’t ignore demand destruction risk when commodity prices rise too quickly.
10. Don’t become emotionally attached to a bullish thesis when market conditions start changing.
11. Focus on the balance sheet, cash flows and people (management) behind those stocks!


Antifragile Thinking: Commodity chemicals and pharma APIs could surprise on the upside. Cyclical, short-term trade. Not a buy and sleep. Buy to sell. Buy fast. Sell faster. Stick to what you understand. Ignore social validation.

items.title: RT Pritesh Gupta: most debates fail before they even begin because the problem is framed wrong from the start. people think they are arguing about sol...
items.description_html:
RT Pritesh Gupta
most debates fail before they even begin because the problem is framed wrong from the start.

people think they are arguing about solutions, but often they are only reacting to symptoms. the real issue stays hidden underneath. a good framework helps you move from what is visible to what is actually causing it.

this is why many indian systems remain stuck. the focus stays on inputs: money spent, schemes launched, announcements made. but inputs are not outcomes. distributing tablets is not the same as children learning. activity gets measured because it is easy to count. outcomes get ignored because they are harder to see.

whoever defines the problem usually controls the solution. frame a learning crisis as an infrastructure issue and the answer becomes buildings and devices. frame it as an information issue and the answer becomes better data, feedback, and accountability. same reality, different response.

that is why framing is not just intellectual, it is political. the way a problem is named decides who gets blamed, what progress looks like, and whose reality matters. which is why getting the question right is often harder, and far more important, than finding an answer.

check out: http://frameworks.pranaykotas.com by @pranaykotas

items.title: SBI 4QFY26 Results: First Cut: Margin slips sharply dragging down profitability *SBI reported 9% PAT miss at ~Rs197bn mainly due to lower NII growth 4...
items.description_html:
SBI 4QFY26 Results: First Cut: Margin slips sharply dragging down profitability

*SBI reported 9% PAT miss at ~Rs197bn mainly due to lower NII growth 4%YoY/-6% QoQ, other income and higher other opex.

* Credit growth was strong and ahead of expectations at 17.2% YoY / 5.4% QoQ, the highest in the last eleven quarters. However, this was mainly driven by corporates and thus hurt loan yields.

* Deposit growth improved to 11% YoY / ~5% QoQ which is ahead of expectations too with stable LDR QoQ at 82%.

* NIM declined by 18bps QoQ to 2.81%, likely due to lower loan yield and higher cost of funds. There is some reclassification of interest income but does not much affects margins.

* Slippages were slightly higher at 0.47% but headline asset quality continued to improve, with 16bps QoQ decline in GNPA ratio to 1.5% (all-time low). NNPA stable QoQ to 0.4% however specific PCR drops by 114 bps to 74%.

* Clarity on margin contraction in 4Q, outlook on growth/margins and forex trade unwinding post RBIs ditkat will be awaited from results

* SBI currently trades at 1.2x FY-28E ABV (ex-subs). Currently, we have a BUY rating on the stock given its strong growth, more than 1% RoA and lower possibility of SBI being part of ensuing PSB consolidation.

items.description_html:
RT Prabhakar Kudva
If you're confused about how to navigate the market, what stocks to buy, what to sell, it is because you dont have a system or an anchor which can guide your decisions.

Newsflow driven investing can drive us crazy and is extremely unprofitable.

A system gives you coherence in thoughts and the clarity to execute. It need not be rigid and its not meant to be machine like. It should more like an anchor around which you can think clearly and decide.

For me the anchors are earnings and growth that drive my actions.

Start systemizing your investing. It's one of the biggest edges out there.

items.title: RT nikhilv: There are a few reasons for this - Inequality literature in Claude's pretraining data is 'actionable', the model sees this as a problem to...
items.description_html:
RT nikhilv
There are a few reasons for this -
Inequality literature in Claude's pretraining data is 'actionable', the model sees this as a problem to be solved. Conversely, literature around growth is framed descriptively, like matter-of-fact. So the model has learnt that anyone serious about public policy will be more concerned about inequality than growth.
Anthropic's constitution is also to blame - it introduces priors, some explicitly mentioned (avoid harms to vulnerable groups/be fair etc - inequality concerns follow naturally). And then you have RLHF from US/UK left of centre cohorts, and black box reveals itself.
Try grok and you'll see a mirror image - xAI tuned the priors the other way, so it may flip rather than disappear @pranaykotas @srajagopalan @amitvarma @AshishPonders @acorn
